January 5, 1978 
Dr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Dear Dr. Lowenstein: 
The Agriculture and Water Resources Committee 
visited Mexico during the week of October 17, 1977 at 
the invitation of the People of Mexico to discuss the 
exportation of Mexican agricultural products to 
California. 
The Members of the Committee paid for their own 
air fare to Mexico, but the Mexican Government supplied 
our food, lodging, and transportation while in Mexico. 
Should we ask them how much they spent on each of us 
and report it, or does this have to be reported? 
There is a certain amount of urgency on this 
opinion since many of the Members are preparing their 
year-end reports. 
I would appreciate hearing from you 
soon. 
Thank you. 
Sincerely, 
RSA:cah 
CHAIRMAN

This is in reference to my January 5, 1978 letter 
(copy enclosed) asking for an opinion on the reporting 
of the trip to Mexico by the Senate Agriculture and 
Water Resources Connnittee. 
We need an answer from you in order to meet the 
deadline on reporting. 
Would you be kind enough to 
send me your response innnediately. 
Thank you.

January 5, 1978 
Dr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman 
 
Dear Dr. Lowenstein: 
The Agriculture and Water Resources Committee 
visited Mexico during the week of October 17, 1977 at 
the invitation of the People of Mexico to discuss the 
exportation of Mexican agricultural products to 
California. 
The Members of the Committee paid for their own 
air fare to Mexico, but the Mexican Government supplied 
our food, lodging, and transportation while in Mexico. 
Should we ask them how much they spent on each of us 
and report it, or does this have to be reported? 
There is a certain amount of urgency on this 
opinion since many of the Members are preparing their 
year-end reports. 
I would appreciate hearing from you 
soon. 
Thank you.
sections.conclusion: You ask whether you must make inquiries to the source of the gift to determine the value of what you use to report on your Statement of Economic Interest. However, it is not necessary to make inquiries to the source of the gift. We believe that if you can make a good faith estimate of the value of the gift that should be reported, you will have met the standard. Of course, if you would rather make inquiry of the donor, that is permissible.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether California State Senators must inquire about and report the value of food, lodging, and transportation provided by the Mexican Government during an official committee visit to Mexico, when the senators paid for their own airfare.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that while senators may inquire about the value of gifts from foreign governments, they are not required to do so; instead, they must report a good faith estimate of the value on their Statement of Economic Interest.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with moderate quality. FPPC response appears to be the upper portion of the document (response letter from FPPC). Multiple versions/copies of the incoming request included. Some text degradation due to OCR but question and conclusion sections are sufficiently legible for extraction.

January 5, 1978 
Dr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman 
 
Dear Dr. Lowenstein: 
The Agriculture and Water Resources Committee 
visited Mexico during the week of October 17, 1977 at 
the invitation of the People of Mexico to discuss the 
exportation of Mexican agricultural products to 
California. 
The Members of the Committee paid for their own 
air fare to Mexico, but the Mexican Government supplied 
our food, lodging, and transportation while in Mexico. 
Should we ask them how much they spent on each of us 
and report it, or does this have to be reported? 
There is a certain amount of urgency on this 
opinion since many of the Members are preparing their 
year-end reports. 
I would appreciate hearing from you 
soon. 
Thank you.

CONCLUSION: You ask whether you must make inquiries to the source of the gift to determine the value of what you use to report on your Statement of Economic Interest. However, it is not necessary to make inquiries to the source of the gift. We believe that if you can make a good faith estimate of the value of the gift that should be reported, you will have met the standard. Of course, if you would rather make inquiry of the donor, that is permissible.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to California State Senator Ruben Ayala regarding disclosure requirements for gifts of food, lodging, and transportation provided by the Mexican Government during an official committee visit in October 1977.
